title: Boat Rental in Piran: Costs, Tips and Best Boats for 2026
html:
Planning a boat rental in Piran gives you access to the clear waters of the Adriatic Sea from the historic port at Punta. Day trips start at around EUR 400 for a small motorboat, and BoatHire24 connects you directly with verified owners for a secure booking experience.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Piran?

Yes, a valid boating licence is required for bareboat charters in Slovenian waters. International licence holders should check reciprocity with local authorities. For skippered charters, the operator provides a qualified captain, so no licence is needed from you. Always confirm the exact requirements with the boat owner on BoatHire24 before booking.

Where to set off in Piran

Piran offers several excellent departure points for your day on the water. The main departure point is Marina Piran, located just west of the old town, with mooring for over 150 vessels. From there you can head to Portorož, a nearby resort with a long pier and shallow bays ideal for swimming. Further along the coast is Izola, a fishing town with a small harbour and restaurant-lined quay. For a longer day out, cross the border to Savudrija in Croatia, about 5 nautical miles south, or head to the picturesque islands of the Brijuni National Park, approximately 12 nautical miles away. The shallow waters around the Seča Peninsula are perfect for anchoring and snorkelling.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Piran?

Boat rental costs in Piran range from EUR 200 for a shared tour to over EUR 3,000 for a crewed yacht. Use the price bands below to estimate your budget:

  • Shared group tour: from EUR 80 per person (EUR 200 total) for a 2-hour coastal trip.
  • Small motorboat (5, 6 guests, half-day, 4 hours): EUR 300, 500.
  • Catamaran (8, 12 guests, full-day, 8 hours): EUR 1,200, 2,000.
  • Crewed motor yacht (10+ guests, full-day): EUR 2,500, 3,500.

For a comprehensive selection of options, you can compare verified boat listings on yacht charter Piran to find the vessel that fits your needs.

Which boat to rent

Choose the right vessel for your group size and preferred activities. Below are the recommended types.

  1. Motorboat (4, 6 guests), Best for a flexible day exploring coves and coastal towns, from EUR 300 for half a day.
  2. Rib tender (up to 8 guests), Ideal for speed and access to shallow anchorages, from EUR 250 for half a day.
  3. Sailboat (6, 10 guests), Perfect for a relaxing cruise with the option to sail, from EUR 500 for half a day.
  4. Catamaran (10, 12 guests), Great for families or groups wanting stability and space, from EUR 1,200 for a full day.
  5. Luxury motor yacht (12+ guests), For a premium experience with crew, such as the Princess V58 listing, from EUR 2,500 for a full day.

When is the best season for boat rental in Piran?

The high season runs from June to September, with July and August being busiest and priciest. The prevailing summer wind is the Maestral, a gentle north-westerly that picks up in the afternoon, ideal for sailing. For lower rates and fewer crowds, consider May or October, though some operators may have limited availability. BoatHire24 lists availability for the entire 2026 season.

Local tip: launch early in the morning from Marina Piran to avoid afternoon winds and enjoy the calm sea around the Strunjan nature reserve.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 200, 3,500 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day (8 hours)
  • Guest capacity: up to 12 people
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Piran

Can I rent a boat in Piran without a licence?

Yes, you can book a skippered charter where a qualified captain handles the boat.

What is the best place to anchor near Piran?

The bay of Portorož and the waters off the Seča peninsula offer sheltered sandy bottoms ideal for anchoring.

Are there any restrictions on crossing into Croatian waters from Piran?

You need to clear customs and immigration when crossing the border, so stick to Slovenian waters unless your charter includes the necessary paperwork.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Piran or list your own. Whether you are exploring the coastline for a day or planning a longer voyage, you can find your ideal vessel on BoatHire24.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Piran and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
